fundamentals.name: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. provides cybersecurity solutions in the United States and internationally. Its unified platform provides cloud-delivered protection of endpoints, cloud workloads, identity, and data through a software as a service (SaaS) subscription-based model. The company offers corporate endpoint and cloud workload security, managed security, security and vulnerability management, IT operations management, identity protection, threat intelligence, data protection, SaaS security posture management, and AI powered workflow automation, and securing generative AI workload services, as well as security orchestration, automation, and response; and security information and event management, and log management services. It primarily sells subscriptions to its Falcon platform and cloud modules. The company was incorporated in 2011 and is headquartered in Austin, Texas.

judgment.summary: High-growth cybersecurity company with strong revenue expansion but currently unprofitable on a net basis and trading at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 1.6%; next earnings date 2026-06-03.
positives: Revenue growth 23.3% YoY — demonstrating strong top-line expansion in a key sector.
Gross margin 74.8% — indicates efficient core service delivery.
Free cash flow (TTM) $1.60B — positive cash generation supports operations.
Analyst growth est, current FY 30.1% — continued strong growth expected.
New integration with Claude Compliance API launched — expanding product ecosystem.
negatives: Forward P/E 104.59x — valuation is very high, pricing in significant future growth.
Profit margin -3.4% — currently unprofitable on a net basis.
Return on equity -4.1% — negative profitability impacting shareholder returns.
Debt/Equity 18.34 — high leverage relative to equity.
Mean target $538.76 is below current spot price — analysts may be cautious on valuation.

news.paragraph: CrowdStrike shares saw a slight dip today despite two analysts from Benchmark and Wedbush raising their price targets to $700, maintaining their coverage. This comes on the heels of a new integration with Claude Compliance API announced yesterday, which aims to enhance security solutions. The mixed price action suggests that while the company is making strategic advancements and receiving positive analyst attention, broader market sentiment or other factors are influencing the stock's immediate movement.
